zotero.abstractNote: Standard width scaling in deep learning, where the learning rate is kept constant as the network width increases, is often considered suboptimal compared to the NTK-parameterization, which requires scaling the learning rate by the width. In this paper, we show that standard width scaling is surprisingly effective. We prove that for a wide range of architectures, standard width scaling leads to a stable training dynamics in the limit of infinite width, provided the learning rate is chosen appropriately. We further show that this limit is not the NTK, but a different, non-linear limit. We provide empirical evidence that this limit is more expressive than the NTK and can achieve better performance on standard benchmarks.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS)
